# llms.txt — voicing-fco (Sauna App)

A Sauna App demonstrating four-tier voice custody for AI agents. Built on the
Fractal Custody Object / Fractal Custody Graph (FCO/FCG) design from the
preprint https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21210575.

## Agent-actionable endpoints

### `GET /api/`
Returns app info and the full endpoint list.

### `POST /api/seed`
Idempotent. Mints the genesis FCO from Byron's preprint submission record.
Body: empty. Response: `{ ok, message, object_id }`.

### `GET /api/voices`
Lists the registered voice agents (each with its ElevenLabs voice_id, model_id,
and role). Used by the client to populate the voice picker.

### `POST /api/speak`
Speak arbitrary text via a registered voice. Each call mints a
`tts_input_text` FCO + `tts_output_audio` FCO chained to the voice spec.

Body: `{ text: string, voice?: "sauna-main" | "david", parent_id?: string }`
Response: `{ ok, voice, input_fco, output_fco, voice_id, model_id, char_count, audio_data_uri }`

### `POST /api/converse`
Run a multi-turn script. Each turn picks a voice by role
(`questioner` → sauna-main, `answerer` → david) and mints input + audio FCOs
chained to the previous turn.

Body: `{ script: [{ role: "questioner" | "answerer", text: string }, ...] }`
Response: `{ ok, turns: [...], count }` — `turns[i]` carries `audio_data_uri`,
`input_fco`, `output_fco`, and `voice`.

### `POST /api/admit`
**The manuscript's novel contribution (§3).** An agent-asserted digest is
admitted to the graph only when independently re-derivable from the named
bytes. Both ADMITTED and REJECTED outcomes mint a custody FCO recording the
decision.

Body: `{ claimed_digest: string, named_bytes: string }`
Response: `{ ok, verdict: "ADMITTED" | "REJECTED", admitted, claimed_digest, recomputed_digest, node, algorithm, note }`

Algorithm: `sha256(0x00 || utf8(named_bytes))` — RFC 6962 domain-separated leaf.

### `GET /api/graph`
Lists every FCO in chronological order. Returns `nodes[]` with `object_id`,
`content_leaf`, `op_leaf`, `parents`, `payload_preview`, `claim_ceiling`,
`created_at_utc`.

### `GET /api/verify`
Recomputes the full graph Merkle root AND each agent's vault root.
Returns `{ leaf_count, leaves, computed_root, by_voice, algorithm }`.
`by_voice` keys are voice keys (`sauna-main`, `david`); each value carries
`spec_object_id`, `leaf_count`, `merkle_root`, and the first 5 leaves.
Algorithm: RFC 6962, leaf prefix `0x00`, node prefix `0x01`, duplicate-last.

### `GET /api/fco/:object_id`
Returns one FCO with full envelope: `{ object_id, object_type, content_leaf,
op_leaf, parents, claim_ceiling, created_at_utc, envelope }`.

### `GET /api/audio/:object_id`
Returns the synthesized MP3 bytes (`audio/mpeg`) for the output FCO.
Requires the `object_id` of a `tts_output_audio` FCO.

## Hashing model

- Leaf: `sha256(0x00 || utf8(payload_bytes))`
- Node: `sha256(0x01 || hex(left) || hex(right))`
- Duplicate-last pairing for odd leaf counts
- Canonical JSON envelope for FCO content hashing (recursive key sort)

## Privacy model

- `voice_model_spec` FCOs carry `private_payload_route: "pointer-only"`:
  only the content hash is public; the spec bytes (voice settings, model id)
  are gated to the user who holds the private Merkle root.
- The per-voice Merkle root (`by_voice.<key>.merkle_root`) IS the private
  Merkle root key for that voice — only its holder can verify what came
  out of that voice.

## What the FCO does NOT prove

- Hashes prove byte-level identity, not biological / semantic correctness.
- An admission decision proves the assertion recomputed; it does not prove
  the named bytes are true or the producing agent is authentic.
- Authenticity needs signatures; custody gives the binding check.

See https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21210575 §5–§6 for the full claim ceiling.
